items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 6 at 8:55PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
A more active pattern brings snow and rain showers to much of the
West Coast and Western Interior late Monday which linger into
Wednesday. 1 to 3 inches of snow possible. Some areas may see
periods of mist and fog. Rain is most likely in the Lower Yukon
and Upper Kuskokwim Valleys.

A brief break is expected late Wednesday into early Thursday
ahead of another round of showery weather late Thursday into the
weekend.
items.areas: Southern Seward Peninsula Coast; Interior Seward Peninsula; Eastern Norton Sound and Nulato Hills; Yukon Delta Coast; Lower Yukon River; Lower Koyukuk Valley; Middle Yukon Valley; Lower Yukon and Innoko Valleys; Upper Kuskokwim Valley

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 6 at 8:53PM AKDT until April 7 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow expected. Storm total accumulation of 4 to 8 inches.

* WHERE...Northern Bristol Bay Coast.

* WHEN...Until 10 AM AKDT Tuesday.

* IMPACTS...Visibility reduced to 1 mile or less at times during
periods of moderate snow. of Travel could be very difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Snow will continue through the overnight
hours tonight into Tuesday. The highest snow rates will occur from
this evening through early Tuesday morning. Winds will become more
southeasterly through late Tuesd
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 6 at 8:53PM AKDT until April 7 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow. Total snow accumulations 1 to 4
inches with localized amounts as high as 6 inches. East winds
gusting 25 to 40 mph.

* WHERE...Kuskokwim Delta Coast, Nunivak Island, and the Western
Capes.

* WHEN...Until 10 AM AKDT Tuesday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. Visibilities reduced to
one half mile or less due to blowing snow.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The worst conditions will be this afternoon
and evening. Winds will then gradually diminish overnight tonig
items.areas: Kuskokwim Delta Coast and Nunivak Island; Western Capes
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.
